title: Executable tools/README.md coverage guard — every dep-map tool must be documented

evidence.ref: Audit found docfresh (added by D025) is in dep map ssot_artifacts but missing from tools/README.md — same recurring drift class as D034. The README is the canonical operator-facing catalog of contracts tools
025-doc-freshness-guard — added docfresh tool but forgot the tools/README.md update; D035 closes the round-trip
034-agent-contract-mirror-guard — same SSOT-mirror-executable pattern; D035 extends it to the tools catalog

guards.ref: internal/ssotdeps.VerifyToolsReadmeCoverage — iterates ssot_artifacts with kind in {verifier, code-generator} and path under tools/. For each, checks tools/README.md contains both `## <name>` heading AND `tools/<name>/main.go` path literal. Wired into ssotdeps cross-check as the 5th sub-check axis
concept-map C-019 — codifies the rule; concept-map bumped v0.22.0 to v0.23.0

examples: After D035: tools/README.md has `## docfresh` section + `tools/docfresh/main.go` in the 'Where each tool's source lives' table. ssotdeps cross-check reports 'all verifier/code-generator tools documented in tools/README.md'.

Simulated removal of docfresh section: 'tools/README.md coverage drift (D035): tool "docfresh" (ssot_artifact "docfresh-tool", path tools/docfresh/main.go) is missing from tools/README.md. Found heading=false, path=false'.
counterexamples: Non-tool ssot_artifacts (concept-map, agent-roles, schemas, ir docs) are out of scope — they live in their own SSOT homes.

tools/devpattern and tools/knowledge are mentioned in tools/README.md but are NOT in the dep map's ssot_artifacts (they're operator-internal, not CI verifiers per D033). The rule fires on dep-map presence, not on disk presence — D033 already covers the symmetric 'workflows reference an unregistered tool' direction.

Heading AND path are required separately so a stray substring of one tool's name in another's prose doesn't false-positive (e.g. 'docfresh' as a word in some other tool's description shouldn't pass the check).
